task_completion.description: Log in to the GeoServer web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoServer web interface is visible and shows the user is logged in as 'admin' (e.g., 'Welcome admin' or a Logout button is present).
The 'Layers' menu item is selected in the left sidebar, and the main panel displays a list of configured layers.
The 'Edit Layer' page is open for the layer named 'ne_countries' (Workspace: 'ne', Store: 'postgis_ne').

task_completion.description: Enter the CQL filter expression to restrict features to South America
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the 'Data' tab of the Edit Layer page, the 'CQL filter' text box (typically under 'Feature Type Details') contains the exact expression: continent = 'South America'.

task_completion.description: Save the layer configuration to make the filter persistent
task_completion.visual_evidence: A green success banner on the Layers page indicating the layer was saved successfully, OR a Layer Preview (OpenLayers map) showing only the 13 South American countries.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual GeoServer web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard GeoServer UI layout, menus, and forms, rather than a text editor, mocked HTML page, or unrelated application.

integrity.description: Verify the filter was applied persistently in the layer configuration, not just as a temporary URL parameter
integrity.visual_evidence: The CQL filter must be entered into the layer's configuration form (Data tab -> CQL filter) and saved, rather than just appending '&CQL_FILTER=...' to a WMS/WFS URL in the browser address bar.

integrity.description: Verify the success state or filter entry is genuine
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the CQL filter box and any success messages use standard GeoServer fonts and styling, with no signs of browser developer tools being used to alter the page text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target layer is 'ne:ne_countries'. The exact CQL filter required is `continent = 'South America'`. The original dataset contains 177 features globally. When the filter is correctly applied, exactly 13 South American countries will be served by the layer (Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Falkland Is., Guyana, Paraguay, Peru, Suriname, Uruguay, Venezuela). If the agent opens a Layer Preview map, only these 13 countries should be visible.
